mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS rate over the trailing three months signals accommodative monetary policy, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supporting a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return frequency in low/falling-rate periods is not significantly above the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration, triggering rapid risk-off repricing in equities over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following a >0.2pp UNRATE jump are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously over a two-quarter window, the joint signal captures broad-based consumer and construction demand strength that predicts sustained S&P 500 gains over the next quarter, whereas either series alone is a noisier and weaker predictor.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Joint HOUST+RSAFS acceleration signal produces 60-day forward return hit-rate no better than either single-series signal alone, or joint Rank IC is not higher than the maximum of the two univariate Rank ICs across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) year-over-year growth deceleration below zero signals a manufacturing-led supply-side slowdown that depresses forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month, but only during periods when the economy is already in an industrial slowdown regime rather than a broad expansion.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following negative INDPRO YoY growth are not significantly lower than baseline unconditional returns (p > 0.05) within industrial slowdown regimes over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which supports a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or PAYEMS growth periods do not show positive-return frequency significantly above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising simultaneously with CPIAUCSL inflation accelerating above trend, the joint tightening-inflation regime signals a policy-driven valuation compression that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, distinct from either series acting alone since rate hikes without inflation pressure are typically pre-emptive and less damaging.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rising
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle amid rising inflation
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-rising and CPIAUCSL-accelerating signal fails to produce 60-day forward negative-return frequency exceeding 55%, or joint Rank IC is not more negative than either univariate signal across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt consumer demand shock that produces immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets reprice near-term earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following a >0.5% RSAFS decline are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial slowdown, a simultaneous deceleration in both industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a compounding real-economy contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter more reliably than either series alone, since combined production and employment weakness indicates broad-based rather than sector-specific stress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth < 0 AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1%
regime_hint: industrial slowdown with weakening labor market
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO-PAYEMS deceleration signal does not produce 60-day forward negative-return frequency exceeding the univariate INDPRO-only signal, or joint Rank IC fails to exceed the maximum univariate Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.
